codeReview.summary: The accessibility feature implementation is well-executed with minimal, targeted changes. The worker correctly identified that the codebase already had strong accessibility foundations (native <button> elements, aria-pressed, focus rings, checkmark icons, line-through, reduced-motion support) and focused on the two real gaps: (1) incorrect ARIA role='grid' on the Board container (changed to role='group' since the board uses Tab navigation, not arrow-key roving tabindex), and (2) missing heading structure (added sr-only h2 above the board for proper h1→h2 hierarchy). The 27-test suite comprehensively covers all 10 VAL-AX assertions.

summary: PASS — Clean, targeted accessibility improvements with no blocking issues. The Board ARIA role was correctly changed from 'grid' to 'group' (Tab navigation doesn't warrant grid semantics), an sr-only h2 heading was added for proper heading hierarchy, and a thorough 27-test suite validates all VAL-AX assertions. All interactive elements are native <button> elements with proper ARIA attributes (aria-pressed, aria-label), visible focus rings, non-color indicators (checkmark icon + line-through), and prefers-reduced-motion support. No keyboard traps exist. Heading structure is logical with single h1 per page.
